title: Ceramic Kiln Multi-Zone Sintering Profile Controller
description: Three-zone alumina kiln controller that executes a programmable multi-segment sintering profile stored in EEPROM. Each zone runs an independent PID loop with a shared profile scheduler. Grain-growth binder-burnout ramp constraints are enforced, faults on TC failure or overtemperature cut all zones, and state is logged via RS232 to supervisory PC.
objective: Track three-zone alumina sintering profile (binder burnout → sinter ramp → 1580 °C dwell → cool); maintain ±5 °C zone uniformity; fault-cut all SSRs on TC error or T > 1650 °C; log every 10 s.
constraints: Arduino Mega; three AD8495 TC conditioners; three Crydom SSRs; AT24C256 EEPROM; RS232; 240 V three-zone kiln; BOM < $100.
supporting_fact_titles: Ceramic Sintering Grain Growth Kinetics and Final Density Evolution
element_usage_notes: Three AD8495 modules on A0/A1/A2; 5 mV/°C → temperature computed in firmware
Each SSR PWM updated independently from its zone PID; all cut to 0 on any fault
Profile segments loaded from EEPROM at setup; segment pointer shared across zones
RS232 frame: epoch, sp, T1, T2, T3, pwm1, pwm2, pwm3, fault_code every 10 s

element_interactions.description: Profile scheduler provides current-segment setpoint to each zone PID; SSR duty set accordingly
Each zone temperature is the PID process variable for its SSR drive output
All three zone temperatures included in RS232 telemetry frame every 10 s
Three SSR duty cycle values logged in telemetry for energy audit
